content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be retrofitted with a self-supervised "failure cache" module that stores and encodes only unproductive, low-reward failed trajectories, using prediction error from these failures as an intrinsic curiosity bonus that outperforms conventional ICM/RND surprise signals. This module prioritizes exploration of gaps in the agent's memory that directly lead to avoidable failure, rather than just any unknown state, turning past mistakes into targeted exploration pressure.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
